use-navigation.name: use-navigation
use-navigation.title: useNavigation
use-navigation.description: Standalone keyboard navigation for role-based lists and tabs

content.text: ARIA role or data-contract item type used for navigation item discovery.
Options for standalone role-based list navigation.
Ref to the container element holding navigable items.
ARIA role used to query navigable children within the container.
Controlled highlight value. When provided, the hook operates in controlled mode.
Initial highlighted value in uncontrolled mode.
Called when the controlled highlight value should change.
Called for Space selection and as the Enter fallback when onEnter is not provided.
Called for Enter selection, overriding the onSelect Enter fallback when provided.
Wrap around when reaching the first or last item.
Whether the navigation hook is active.
Call preventDefault() on handled keyboard events.
* Called when the user tries to navigate past the first or last item.
* Receives the direction, originating event, and key that hit the boundary.
Custom key names to move highlight up or left.
Custom key names to move highlight down or right.
Navigation axis for default arrow keys.
Skip aria-disabled, data-disabled, and native disabled items during navigation.
* Move DOM focus to the next item. When false, focus stays on the composite owner, which should
* expose the highlighted option through aria-activedescendant.
Ignore items owned by nested composite containers such as nested listboxes.
Advanced owner selector override for roles without a standard composite owner.
The value of the currently highlighted item, or null.
Returns true if the given value is the highlighted item.
Imperatively set the highlighted item. Pass null to clear.
Keyboard event handler to attach to the container element.
* Adds standalone keyboard navigation, selection, and focus tracking to a
* role-based list. Attach the returned `onKeyDown` handler to the container.
Editable non-item bubbling into a wrapper that owns the items: let native handle it.
Don't swallow Enter/Space/Home/End for a non-navigation control beside the list.

* Receives the direction, originating event, and key that hit the boundary.
Custom key names to move highlight up or left.
Custom key names to move highlight down or right.
Navigation axis for default arrow keys.

* Move DOM focus to the next item. When false, focus stays on the composite owner, which should
* expose the highlighted option through aria-activedescendant.
Ignore items owned by nested composite containers such as nested listboxes.
Advanced owner selector override for roles without a standard composite owner.
The value of the currently highlighted item, or null.
Returns true if the given value is the highlighted item.
Imperatively set the highlighted item. Pass null to clear.
Keyboard event handler to attach to the container element.
* Adds standalone keyboard navigation, selection, and focus tracking to a
* role-based list. Attach the returned `onKeyDown` handler to the container.
Editable non-item bubbling into a wrapper that owns the items: let native handle it.
Don't swallow Enter/Space/Home/End for a non-navigation control beside the list.
* Shared list-navigation state machine used by the standalone and provider-backed
DOM boundary: data-value is opaque to TS; consumers parameterize TValue.
Native disabled controls can't take DOM focus; report failure to step past.
DOM boundary: data-value is opaque to TS; consumers parameterize TValue.
Bounded by item count so an all-disabled wrap list can't loop forever.
Resolves default previous/next keys for a vertical or horizontal list.
* Dispatches one navigation key to movement, edge, and activation callbacks.
* Returns whether the key was handled.
Step forward so a native-disabled first item is skipped, matching arrow stepping.
Data attribute used by @diffgazer/keys to mark navigable DOM items.
Navigation item types recognized by DOM query helpers and navigation hooks.
Query used to discover navigable items inside a container.
Item role or data-contract type to query.
* Exclude items that expose or inherit a disabled state: aria-disabled,
* data-disabled, or native disabled on the item or an ancestor.
Exclude items owned by nested composite containers.
Override the composite owner selector used for scoping, or null to disable owner scoping.
True when an aria-disabled/data-disabled ancestor (not the element itself) disables the item.
Merged results from multiple selectors may interleave; restore DOM order.
* Finds navigable descendants matching the role/data contract in DOM order.
* Items hidden by `hidden`, `inert`, or `aria-hidden="true"` (self or ancestor,
* across shadow boundaries) are always excluded because they are not
* accessibility-reachable. Disabled items and items under an
* aria-disabled/data-disabled ancestor are skipped by default.
Finds one navigable item by its `data-value`.
Returns the public data attributes needed for role-independent navigation.
Returns the `data-value` for the navigable item containing DOM focus.
* Moves DOM focus to a navigable item by value, with optional first/last
* fallback, and returns the focused value.
Vertical movement direction derived from ArrowUp/ArrowDown or boundaries.
Orientation-neutral boundary direction emitted by navigation hooks.
Returns true for keys handled by the list-navigation primitives.
Maps ArrowUp/ArrowDown to a semantic vertical direction.
Converts a previous/next boundary to up/down.
Converts a boundary to up/down only when the triggering key was vertical.
area is display:none by default in every UA stylesheet yet remains
focusable via its own href; only its ancestors' display can hide it.
Per ARIA, aria-hidden="false" does NOT re-expose content hidden by an aria-hidden="true" ancestor.
* Returns false when a hidden, inert, or aria-hidden="true" self-or-ancestor
* (across shadow boundaries) removes the element from keyboard reach. Shared
* with navigation discovery so it skips the same unreachable items focus does.
Descendants of the first <legend> are not disabled per spec; keep searching upward.
* Returns true for visible, non-disabled elements that can receive programmatic
jsdom returns compound-selector matches out of tree order; normalize.
Sort comparator that returns elements in composed order across open shadow roots.
* Returns all focusable descendants in DOM order, including programmatic focus
* targets that are not reachable by Tab.
* Returns browser Tab-order descendants, excluding negative tabindex targets
* and collapsing native radio groups to one Tab stop.
Returns the first focusable descendant in DOM order.
Returns true when the element contains its owner document's deep active element, across shadow roots.
Returns the `Window` realm for a DOM value, or null for non-DOM values.
Realm-safe HTMLElement guard for elements from iframes or other documents.
Realm-safe Node guard using a known owner window.
Returns the shadow host when a node lives in a shadow root, else null.
Returns the deepest active element, descending through open shadow roots.
Returns true when `container` contains `target` in the composed tree, across shadow boundaries.
Returns the nearest self-or-ancestor matching `selector` in the composed tree, across shadow boundaries.
* Returns the deepest composed event target, past the retargeted shadow host.
* Returns true for form-like or editable elements that can own keyboard input,
* including select and non-text input types.
* Returns true only for enabled text-editable targets that should keep typing

use-focus-restore.name: use-focus-restore
use-focus-restore.title: useFocusRestore
use-focus-restore.description: Capture and restore focus around overlays and temporary UI with nested stack safety

content.text: Options for capturing and restoring focus around temporary UI.
Whether capture and restore are active.
Restore focus during cleanup if capture was called and restore was not called manually.
Fallback element to focus when the captured target is unavailable.
Stores the current focus target for the provided document.
Focuses the captured or fallback target and returns whether focus moved.
The last captured focus target, or null when nothing is captured.
* Captures the current focus target and restores it later, with per-document
Latest-ref sync: stable focus callbacks read optionsRef, so it must update every render by design.
Options for capturing and restoring focus around temporary UI.
Whether capture and restore are active.
Restore focus during cleanup if capture was called and restore was not called manually.
Fallback element to focus when the captured target is unavailable.
Stores the current focus target for the provided document.
Focuses the captured or fallback target and returns whether focus moved.
The last captured focus target, or null when nothing is captured.
* Captures the current focus target and restores it later, with per-document
Latest-ref sync: stable focus callbacks read optionsRef, so it must update every render by design.
Options shared by the imperative focus-restore utilities.
Passes `preventScroll` to the focus call when restoring focus.
* Returns the current restorable focus target, ignoring body, documentElement,
* Focuses a connected target and returns whether the document's active element
Older engines / some jsdom versions reject the FocusOptions argument; fall back to plain focus().
Returns the `Window` realm for a DOM value, or null for non-DOM values.
Realm-safe HTMLElement guard for elements from iframes or other documents.
Realm-safe Node guard using a known owner window.
Returns the shadow host when a node lives in a shadow root, else null.
Returns the deepest active element, descending through open shadow roots.
Returns true when `container` contains `target` in the composed tree, across shadow boundaries.
Returns the nearest self-or-ancestor matching `selector` in the composed tree, across shadow boundaries.
* Returns the deepest composed event target, past the retargeted shadow host.
* Returns true for form-like or editable elements that can own keyboard input,
* including select and non-text input types.
* Returns true only for enabled text-editable targets that should keep typing

use-focus-trap.name: use-focus-trap
use-focus-trap.title: useFocusTrap
use-focus-trap.description: Trap Tab focus within a container element

content.text: Options for trapping Tab focus inside a container.
Element that receives focus when the trap activates.
Restore focus to the previously focused element when the trap releases.
Whether the focus trap is active.
* Keeps Tab and Shift+Tab focus inside a container while active, with nested
* trap stacking and optional focus restoration on release. Active traps listen
* for keydown and focusin in the capture phase on the container's owner document.
Detach listeners BEFORE focus moves out in release(), or the document-level
focusin recapture re-traps the restored target. restoreOnUnmount:false keeps
this stack hook from moving focus during its own unmount cleanup.
No dependency array on purpose: React does not re-fire effects when
containerRef.current mutates while the ref object stays stable.
restoreFocus is release-time policy; update in place instead of tearing
down (which would recapture the interior as the restore target, not the opener).
Resolve the observer BEFORE any side effect (focus capture, tabindex mutation)
so a document without MutationObserver bails out leaving the container untouched.
Capture the opener before focus moves inside, so a false-to-true toggle can still restore to it.
Push BEFORE arming/focusing: pushTrap suspends the previous top so its
focusin handler does not recapture focus away from this new trap.
Options for trapping Tab focus inside a container.
Element that receives focus when the trap activates.
Restore focus to the previously focused element when the trap releases.
Whether the focus trap is active.
* Keeps Tab and Shift+Tab focus inside a container while active, with nested
* trap stacking and optional focus restoration on release. Active traps listen
* for keydown and focusin in the capture phase on the container's owner document.
Detach listeners BEFORE focus moves out in release(), or the document-level
focusin recapture re-traps the restored target. restoreOnUnmount:false keeps
this stack hook from moving focus during its own unmount cleanup.
No dependency array on purpose: React does not re-fire effects when

restoreFocus is release-time policy; update in place instead of tearing
down (which would recapture the interior as the restore target, not the opener).
Resolve the observer BEFORE any side effect (focus capture, tabindex mutation)
so a document without MutationObserver bails out leaving the container untouched.
Capture the opener before focus moves inside, so a false-to-true toggle can still restore to it.
Push BEFORE arming/focusing: pushTrap suspends the previous top so its
focusin handler does not recapture focus away from this new trap.

use-scroll-lock.name: use-scroll-lock
use-scroll-lock.title: useScrollLock
use-scroll-lock.description: Prevent body/element scrolling with reference counting

content.text: Options for preventing body or element scrolling.
Ref to the element to lock. Defaults to `document.body` when omitted.
Whether the scroll lock is active.
Effect-on-every-render is intentional: React does not re-fire effects when
target.current mutates while the ref object stays stable.
* Locks scrolling by mutating the target element's overflow and padding, with
* scrollbar-width compensation and shared reference counting. It installs no
No dependency array on purpose; see hook-level comment above.
Options for preventing body or element scrolling.
Ref to the element to lock. Defaults to `document.body` when omitted.
Whether the scroll lock is active.
Effect-on-every-render is intentional: React does not re-fire effects when
target.current mutates while the ref object stays stable.
* Locks scrolling by mutating the target element's overflow and padding, with
* scrollbar-width compensation and shared reference counting. It installs no
No dependency array on purpose; see hook-level comment above.
